R Notebook

This is an R Markdown Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code.

Try executing this chunk by clicking the Run button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing Ctrl+Shift+Enter.

library(zoo)

ds <- read.csv("ai_workforce.csv")

colSums(is.na(ds))
                            Year                          Country 
                              13                                0 
        AI_Investment_BillionUSD          Automation_Rate_Percent 
                              11                               12 
         Employment_Rate_Percent               Average_Salary_USD 
                              15                               17 
              Productivity_Index Reskilling_Investment_MillionUSD 
                              24                               19 
                 AI_Policy_Index         Job_Displacement_Million 
                              15                               15 
            Job_Creation_Million               AI_Readiness_Score 
                              22                               17 
ds$Automation_Rate_Percent[is.na(ds$Automation_Rate_Percent)] <- mean(ds$Automation_Rate_Percent, na.rm = TRUE)
ds$Employment_Rate_Percent[is.na(ds$Employment_Rate_Percent)] <- mean(ds$Employment_Rate_Percent, na.rm = TRUE)
ds$Productivity_Index[is.na(ds$Productivity_Index)] <- mean(ds$Productivity_Index, na.rm = TRUE)
ds$Reskilling_Investment_MillionUSD[is.na(ds$Reskilling_Investment_MillionUSD)] <- mean(ds$Reskilling_Investment_MillionUSD, na.rm = TRUE)
ds$AI_Investment_BillionUSD[is.na(ds$AI_Investment_BillionUSD)] <- median(ds$AI_Investment_BillionUSD, na.rm = TRUE)
ds$Reskilling_Investment_MillionUSD[is.na(ds$Reskilling_Investment_MillionUSD)] <- median(ds$Reskilling_Investment_MillionUSD, na.rm = TRUE)
ds$AI_Policy_Index <- NULL
ds$Job_Creation_Million[is.na(ds$Job_Creation_Million)] <- median(ds$Job_Creation_Million, na.rm = TRUE)
ds$Job_Displacement_Million[is.na(ds$Job_Displacement_Million)] <- median(ds$Job_Displacement_Million, na.rm = TRUE)
ds$AI_Readiness_Score[is.na(ds$AI_Readiness_Score)] <- median(ds$AI_Readiness_Score, na.rm = TRUE)
ds$Average_Salary_USD[is.na(ds$Average_Salary_USD)] <- median(ds$Average_Salary_USD, na.rm = TRUE)

ds$Country[ds$Country == ""] <- NA
ds$Country <- na.locf(ds$Country, na.rm = FALSE)
ds$Country <- na.locf(ds$Country, fromLast = TRUE)

ds$Year <- rep(2015:2025, times = length(unique(ds$Country)))

colSums(is.na(ds))
                            Year                          Country 
                               0                                0 
        AI_Investment_BillionUSD          Automation_Rate_Percent 
                               0                                0 
         Employment_Rate_Percent               Average_Salary_USD 
                               0                                0 
              Productivity_Index Reskilling_Investment_MillionUSD 
                               0                                0 
        Job_Displacement_Million             Job_Creation_Million 
                               0                                0 
              AI_Readiness_Score 
                               0 
avg_auto <- aggregate(Automation_Rate_Percent ~ Year, data = ds, mean)

plot(avg_auto$Year, avg_auto$Automation_Rate_Percent, type = "l",
     xlab = "Year",
     ylab = "Automation Rate (%)",
     main = "Automation Growth Over Time")


avg_emp <- aggregate(Employment_Rate_Percent ~ Year, data = ds, mean)

plot(avg_emp$Year, avg_emp$Employment_Rate_Percent, type = "l",
     xlab = "Year",
     ylab = "Employment Rate (%)",
     main = "Employment Trend Over Time")


plot(ds$Automation_Rate_Percent,
     ds$Average_Salary_USD,
     main = "Impact of Automation on Salaries",
     xlab = "Automation Rate (%)",
     ylab = "Average Salary (USD)",
     pch = 19,
     col = "blue")

model <- lm(Average_Salary_USD ~ Automation_Rate_Percent, data = ds)
abline(model, col = "red", lwd = 2)


countries <- c("United States", "China", "India", "Germany", "UK")
small_ds <- subset(ds, Country %in% countries)

plot(NULL,
     xlim = range(small_ds$Year),
     ylim = range(small_ds$Job_Creation_Million),
     xlab = "Year",
     ylab = "Job Creation (Million)",
     main = "Job Creation in Selected Countries")

cols <- c("blue", "red", "green", "purple", "orange")

i <- 1
for (c in countries) {
  temp <- subset(small_ds, Country == c)
  
  lines(temp$Year, temp$Job_Creation_Million,
        col = cols[i], lwd = 2)
  
  i <- i + 1
}

legend("topleft",
       legend = countries,
       col = cols,
       lty = 1)



plot(ds$Reskilling_Investment_MillionUSD,
     ds$Employment_Rate_Percent,
     main = "Reskilling vs Employment",
     xlab = "Reskilling Investment",
     ylab = "Employment Rate (%)",
     pch = 19)

abline(lm(Employment_Rate_Percent ~ Reskilling_Investment_MillionUSD, data = ds),
       col = "red")


boxplot(Automation_Rate_Percent ~ Country,
        data = ds,
        main = "Automation by Country",
        xlab = "Country",
        ylab = "Automation Rate (%)")


robots_year <- aggregate(Robots_Adopted ~ Year, data = robotics, mean)

plot(robots_year$Year, robots_year$Robots_Adopted,
     type = "l",
     xlab = "Year",
     ylab = "Robots Adopted",
     main = "Robotics Adoption Over Time",
     col = "blue",
     lwd = 2)


robots_ind <- aggregate(Robots_Adopted ~ Industry, data = robotics, sum)

barplot(robots_ind$Robots_Adopted,
        names.arg = robots_ind$Industry,
        col = "skyblue",
        main = "Robots Adoption by Industry",
        xlab = "Industry",
        ylab = "Total Robots Adopted",
        las = 2)


jobs_ind <- aggregate(Jobs_Displaced ~ Industry, data = robotics, sum)

pie(jobs_ind$Jobs_Displaced,
    labels = jobs_ind$Industry,
    main = "Jobs Displaced by Industry")


jobs_year <- aggregate(Jobs_Displaced ~ Year, data = robotics, mean)

plot(jobs_year$Year, jobs_year$Jobs_Displaced,
     type = "l",
     xlab = "Year",
     ylab = "Jobs Displaced by robots",
     main = "Jobs Displacement Over Time",
     col = "red",
     lwd = 2)
